items.headline: Cupertino updates Health and Safety element of General Plan
items.summary: The City Council reviewed updates to the Health and Safety Element of the Cupertino General Plan covering 2015–2040. This foundational policy document shapes how the city addresses public health, emergency preparedness, and safety infrastructure for the next decade-plus.

items.headline: City Council appoints permanent City Manager
items.summary: The City Council adopted a resolution appointing a permanent City Manager. This is a significant leadership decision for the city's executive administration.

items.headline: Duplex permitted on Old Piedmont Road in San José
items.summary: A new two-story duplex with 2 units received a building permit at 2174 Old Piedmont Road, representing a notable housing addition in a city facing significant housing demand. At over $1 million, it's the largest residential project in this permit batch.

items.headline: Sunnyvale updates water shortage plan as drought pressures mount
items.summary: The Sustainability Commission is reviewing the draft 2025 Urban Water Master Plan and Water Shortage Contingency Plan on May 18. The contingency plan outlines how the city will manage water supplies during shortages, relevant as California continues to face drought conditions.

items.headline: City proposes residential electrification program framework
items.summary: Palo Alto is considering proposed principles for residential electrification programs to help the city comply with air quality district regulations. The council will review the status and framework of these programs on May 15, 2026.

items.headline: City updates rules for accessory and junior dwelling units
items.summary: Palo Alto is amending its municipal code to address changes in state law regarding accessory dwelling units (ADUs) and junior ADUs. The ordinance updates Chapters 16, 18, and 21 of the city code to align with new state requirements and city council direction.

items.headline: Stream corridor protection rules being reconsidered
items.summary: The city council is considering amendments to Section 18.40.140 of the Palo Alto Municipal Code, which governs stream corridor protection. The specific changes were not detailed in the agenda.

items.headline: Auto Sales Use Approved at El Camino Real Commercial Site
items.summary: The Planning Commission considered a special development permit to allow auto sales at 975 W. El Camino Real, an existing commercial property in the ECR-C zone. The project involves site repaving, restriping, and landscape upgrades with no building expansion or exterior modifications.
